id.description: Stable identifier. Historically strings; SkiWatch source uses integers, so both are accepted.

name_i18n.description: Locale-keyed names matching SkiWatch's LocalizedText shape.

difficulty.description: Unified international difficulty band. Korean resorts label these as 초급/중급/상급/최상급 (signed as one black / two black for advanced/expert); North American resorts use green/blue/black/double-black. The data uses the international name; consumers map to local labelling at render time.

oneOf.description: Bunny hill — beginner area at the base, often a magic carpet. KR: 초보.
Easiest groomed run. NA: green circle. KR: 초급.
Stepping-stone band between beginner and intermediate. KR: 초중급.
Standard cruising run. NA: blue square. KR: 중급.
Stepping-stone band between intermediate and advanced. KR: 중상급.
Demanding groomed or moguled run. NA: single black diamond. KR: 상급, signed as one black.
Steep / technical / off-piste-adjacent. NA: double black diamond. KR: 최상급, signed as two black.
Competition / extreme — beyond expert public-skier scope.
Features (rails, jumps, halfpipe). Difficulty varies independently of the slope band.
Off-piste / non-patrolled / requires gear and judgement.
